intended_use: The CLARUS 700 ophthalmic camera is indicated to capture, display, annotate and store images to aid in the diagnosis and monitoring of diseases and disorders occurring in the retina, ocular surface an
intended_use_full: The CLARUS 700 ophthalmic camera is indicated to capture, display, annotate and store images to aid in the diagnosis and monitoring of diseases and disorders occurring in the retina, ocular surface and visible adnexa. It provides true color and autofluorescence imaging modes for stereo, widefield, ultra-widefield, and montage fields of view. The CLARUS 700 angiography is indicated as an aid in the visualization of vascular structures of the retina and choroid.
target_condition: Retinal and choroidal vascular disorders; diabetic retinopathy (ETDRS grading support); retinal diseases and disorders
ground_truth: Independent grading of image quality for angiography images
other_metrics: Image quality grading by three independent graders for simultaneous FA, simultaneous ICGA, and standalone ICGA; graders determined images were clinically acceptable
